c1.speaker: prudence
c1.line: Twenty years, Mr. Gibbons, they have called it my "little theory." Theodora Vale and Jonah Croft. Rival families, one river between their houses, and letters — I have thirty-one of them — that would make a stone weep.

c2.speaker: prudence
c2.line: The last letter, the one where she ends it or doesn't, Theodora sealed into the capsule herself. Fifty years I couldn't touch it; three days I've been unable to sleep for wanting to. So yes. Suspect me. I would suspect me.

c3.speaker: gary
c3.line: Where were you, night before last-but-one?

c4.speaker: narrator
c4.line: A beat; the faintest color rises along the heron's grey.

c5.speaker: prudence
c5.line: Cataloguing. With Evelyn Croft. Until three in the morning. We have been... reconstructing both sides of the correspondence. You may verify with the florist. Bring context; she is armed with shears.
